fresh_facts.claim: The USS Boxer amphibious ready group, including a Marine Expeditionary Unit, deployed from San Diego on March 18, 2026, en route to relieve the USS Tripoli at Diego Garcia.
The Pentagon plans to deploy 3,000-4,000 soldiers from the 82nd Airborne Division to unspecified Middle East locations for regional capacity buildup as of March 24, 2026.
Approximately 2,500 Marines are deploying with warships including USS Boxer and escorts to address Strait of Hormuz threats and oil infrastructure as of March 20, 2026.
Approximately 50,000 US troops were in the region prior to recent deployments, with 13 killed and 290 wounded in the conflict as of March 24, 2026.
The US has struck approximately 9,000 targets in Iran since February 28, 2026, focusing on missiles, navy, and infrastructure under Operation Epic Fury.
The conflict began on February 28, 2026, and had entered its fourth week as of late March 2026.
Iran's March 25 ceasefire proposal demanded war reparations, an end to hostilities, and retained Strait of Hormuz control.
The US ceasefire position sought nuclear rollback, IAEA monitoring, missile limits, and sanctions relief from Iran.
President Trump threatened to 'unleash hell' if no ceasefire deal is reached with Iran as of March 2026.
Iran dismissed US ceasefire talks as 'negotiating with itself' as of March 2026.
Thailand secured safe passage for its oil vessels through the Strait of Hormuz on March 28, 2026.
The Trump administration extended a pause on Iranian energy strikes by 10 days as of March 28, 2026.
IRGC hardliners have come to dominate Iranian leadership following the killing of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and other leadership losses.
Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr was named Secretary of Supreme National Security Council on March 28, 2026, shifting security decisions from government to military structures.
Iran-Iraq war-era IRGC figures have been appointed to key roles in the Iranian government as of March 2026.
No evidence of rapid regime fracture or defections has been reported despite strikes on Iranian leadership facilities as of March 2026.
A nationwide internet blackout has persisted in Iran, tightening government control as of March 2026.
US strikes have caused jailbreaks in Iranian facilities, such as in Mariwan, but no broader civil unrest has been reported as of late March 2026.
The European Parliament raised concerns on March 28, 2026, against promoting specific Iranian opposition leaders.
US strikes have expanded to include targets in Yemen, Iraq, and Gulf shipping as of March 28, 2026.
CENTCOM noted Iranian military capability degradation as a result of US military strikes as of March 2026.
Oil prices have remained elevated due to disruptions to Strait of Hormuz shipping as of March 2026.
US military strikes have targeted Iranian nuclear, military, and industrial sites, degrading missile and naval capabilities as of March 2026.
Kurdish militias in Iraq have eyed cross-border action against Iran but lack sufficient support as of March 2026.

regime_fall.current_status: U.S. and Israeli air campaign has struck approximately 9,000 Iranian targets with operations nearing completion, while major amphibious and ground force deployments position approximately 50,000 total U.S. troops for potential ground operations; the military-dominated regime under IRGC leadership continues rejecting ceasefire terms despite massive capability degradation, nationwide internet blackout, and depleted counterattacks, though a 10-day pause on energy strikes and diplomatic engagement persist.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr appointed Secretary of Supreme National Security Council on March 28, 2026, consolidating military control over security decisions as IRGC hardliners dominate Iranian leadership following Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei's death.
